'''''Boba Fett: Murder Most Foul''''' is the third issue in the [[Star Wars: Boba Fett (comic series)|Boba Fett]] series of one-shot comic books. It was written by [[John Wagner]] and illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]]. It was released on [[August 13]], [[1997]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

==Publisher's summary==
''Gorga may want to call off the hit—the target being his father-in-law and Gorga's wife being suddenly pregnant and all—but Boba Fett was promised a contract, and he doesn't do last-minute cancellations. It's a knockdown, drag-out roustabout in'' Boba Fett: Murder Most Foul!

==Plot summary==
[[Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo/Legends|Gorga Desilijic Aarrpo]] is feuding with his father-in-law [[Orko]] and hires [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to kill him. When Gorga learns that his wife is pregnant and that anything upsetting might threaten the [[Hutt/Legends|Huttlet]], he wants to stop Fett. Not intending to be an assassin, Fett breaks into Orko's lair with non-lethal force to explain to Orko that his life is in danger and to offer protection services. That night, Orko is killed by [[Ry-Kooda]]. Intent on revenge, Ry-Kooda and his [[Boz (Lizling)|side-kick]] go after Gorga and his wife, when Fett intervenes and blasts Ry-Kooda to pieces. Gorga and his wife are reconciled and the little side-kick vows revenge on Fett.
